name: Envelope (AR/AD)
description:
This algorithm generates simple attack/release or attack/decay envelopes. It is based on the disting mk4 algorithm “E-1 AR Envelope”.

In the disting mk4 version the envelope times were always set together from a single knob; this version adds the option of setting them independently.

The envelope times and shapes are set globally; each output channel can however be independently scaled and offset.

In “Attack/release” mode, the envelope will rise to full level and stay there as long as the input is high. In “Attack/decay” mode, the envelope will execute one full attack/decay cycle in response to a trigger input. In “Looped AD” mode, the envelope will continually execute attack/decay cycles as long as the trigger input is high.

In “Macro (asymmetric)” mode, the “Joint time” parameter sweeps from short A & D, through short A & long D, through long A & D, through long A & short D, and finally back to short A & D. In “Macro (symmetric)” mode, the “Joint time” parameter sets the A & D times to the same value, from very short times (about 10ms) to very long times (about 8s). Note that the actual times are heavily dependent on the shape parameters.
gui_description: The display shows the envelope shape. If the visible parameter page is one of the Channel pages, it also shows the current envelope level.

specifications.name: Channels
specifications.description: The number of envelopes to generate.

parameters.name: Trigger mode
parameters.description: The trigger mode. The options are: Attack/release, Attack/decay, Looped AD

parameters.name: Time mode
parameters.description: The envelope time mode. The options are: Macro (asymmetric), Macro (symmetric), Independent

parameters.name: Joint time
parameters.description: Sets the attack and release times if the ‘Time mode’ is one of the two ‘Macro’ options.

parameters.name: Attack time
parameters.description: Sets the attack time if the ‘Time mode’ is ‘Independent’.

parameters.name: Release time
parameters.description: Sets the release time if the ‘Time mode’ is ‘Independent’.

parameters.name: Attack shape
parameters.description: Sets the attack shape, from an exaggerated exponential curve at 0 to an almost linear shape at 128.

parameters.name: Release shape
parameters.description: Sets the release shape, from an exaggerated exponential curve at 0 to an almost linear shape at 128.

parameters.name: Output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above.

parameters.name: Amplitude
parameters.description: Sets the amplitude of the envelope. Note that negative values give an inverted envelope.

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.description: Sets a constant voltage offset for the envelope.

parameters.name: MIDI channel
parameters.description: The MIDI channel on which notes can trigger the envelope.

parameters.name: I2C channel
parameters.description: The I2C channel on which notes can trigger the envelope.

parameters.name: Note number
parameters.description: The MIDI or I2C note number that will trigger the envelope, or ‘-1’ for ‘Any’.

parameters.name: Velocity depth
parameters.description: The amount by which the trigger velocity affects the envelope scale (applies only to MIDI and I2C).
